refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under defined network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment of teleoperation data rate.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and teleoperation control data.
Dependency on network link quality and safety alert timing.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions allow for adaptive protocol effectiveness.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions impacting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating latency monitoring as part of the DP to mitigate teleoperation responsiveness risk.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software module responsible for teleoperation control and communication management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics and packet loss status.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope but less focused on teleoperation communication specifics.
Physical vehicle components have minimal direct role in communication protocol.
Primary module for teleoperation communication management, high containment score.
Safety services may consume communication quality data but do not manage it.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-critical alerts depend on communication quality.
Risk of module overload if Teleoperation module also handles other complex functions.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate interface boundaries with Safety Services to ensure clear separation of communication quality monitoring and safety alerting.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear mechanism to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if communication quality logic is duplicated in Safety Services or NGHE Solution modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and appropriate interface usage.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface from the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for communication quality to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface contracts between Teleoperation and Safety Services to avoid overlapping responsibilities.
Consider adding latency monitoring to DP and IM to address teleoperation responsiveness risk.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns it to an appropriate IM with clear interfaces and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs for further detail.

assumptions: Network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Adaptive communication protocol can meet packet loss targets without excessive latency.
open_risks: Latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness due to retransmissions.
Potential interface overlap with Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Performance data of adaptive communication protocol under expected network conditions.
Interface definition documents between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
